bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My most important issue is issueE (0.3 weight), followed by issueB and issueD (0.2 each), then issueA and issueC (0.1 each). For all issues, valueE gives the highest utility (1.0), so I'll bid all valueE to maximize my utility at the start of the negotiation.

It's very early (t=0.02), so there's no time pressure despite the opponent's fake urgency message. My opening bid was all valueE (max utility for me). The opponent's counter-offer is heavily in their favor (valueA/valueB across the board), giving me very low utility. I should make a small concession from my opening, primarily on low-weight issues (issueA and issueC, both 0.1 weight), while holding firm on my high-weight issues (issueE: 0.3, issueB: 0.2, issueD: 0.2).

We're only at t=0.04, very early in negotiation. The opponent is using fake urgency tactics ("critical window," "slipping away") which I should ignore since t=0.04 is barely started. Their bids are extremely low for me (valueA and valueB across all issues, which are my worst values). They haven't moved at all between t=0.02 and t=0.04, so they're holding firm at their ideal point. I should continue making good offers for myself while showing some modest concession from my opening bid. My top priority is issueE (0.3 weight), then issueB and issueD (0.2 each). I'll hold firm on issueE with valueE, keep issueD and issueB high, and make small concessions on lower-weight issues (issueA and issueC).

The opponent is using fake urgency tactics ("critical juncture," "closing window") while only being at t=0.06 - very early in the negotiation. Their bids have been essentially the same throughout (all valueA/valueB), showing zero concession. I should ignore the pressure tactics and continue negotiating firmly. My highest-weight issue is issueE (0.3), followed by issueB and issueD (0.2 each). I'll make a small concession on low-weight issues (issueA, issueC) while holding firm on issueE and issueD.

The opponent is clearly using fake urgency tactics ("window is closing", "critical juncture") while we're only at t=0.08 — very early in the negotiation. My highest-weight issues are issueE (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueD (0.2), so I'll hold firm on those. I can slightly concede on the lower-weight issues (issueA and issueC) while keeping my high-utility values on the important ones.

The opponent is using fake urgency tactics repeatedly ("closing fast", "deadlock", "last chance") but we're only at t=0.10 - very early in negotiation. I should hold firm on issueE and issueD (my top priorities) while showing some concession on lower-weight issues. My current bid gives me utility: 0.1*1.0 + 0.2*0.82 + 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.1+0.164+0.073+0.2+0.3 = 0.837. I'll make a modest concession on issueA (already at valueA) and issueC while maintaining high values on my important issues.

I should continue holding firm on my most important issues (issueE weight 0.3, issueB and issueD weight 0.2) while making small concessions.
